responseMessageContents.text: Let me start by reading the project skill to unlock the createApp tool.

output.content:
1|---
     2|name: project
     3|description: To set up a new app project. You have to open this skill before setting up a new project. Also contains documentation for generating image assets.
     4|---
     5|
     6|## How to set up a new project
     7|
     8|You just unlocked the `createApp` tool. On the **first message** when the workspace is empty, you MUST call `createApp` before writing any code.
     9|
    10|Call it with:
    11|- `name`: A short, user-friendly app name (e.g. "My Fitness Tracker")
    12|- `framework`: The framework for the project (`"react-native"` or `"swift"`)
    13|- `path`: A short, lowercase folder name for the app. Pick a simple, descriptive name that matches what the app is:
    14|  - `expo` for React Native / Expo apps
    15|  - `ios` for native iOS / Swift apps
    16|  - `admin` for admin panels / dashboards
    17|  - `api` for backend APIs
    18|  - Use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. No spaces or special characters.
    19|
    20|This writes the template files into a subfolder and creates a root `rork.json` manifest. After it completes, you can start editing code.
    21|
    22|## Image and asset generation
    23|
    24|**To generate app icons or App Store screenshots**, you MUST read `skills/image-gen/SKILL.md` first. It unlocks the `generateImage`, `generateIcon`, and `generateScreenshot` tools with full instructions on how to use them.
    25|
    26|### How to generate image assets (backgrounds, illustrations, UI graphics)
    27|
    28|Use the `generateImageAsset` tool for image assets. Do NOT use `generateImage` — that tool is only for app icons.
    29|
    30|Generate custom image assets using AI (OpenAI gpt-image-1.5). Returns image URLs hosted on R2.
    31|
    32|**Workflow:**
    33|1. Decide what images the app needs (backgrounds, illustrations, banners, etc.)
    34|2. Call `listExistingImageAssets` to check for duplicates
    35|3. Call `generateImageAsset` with `runInBackground: true` for each image
    36|4. Continue building the app — use the returned URL to reference images in code
    37|5. At the end, call `waitImageAssetResult` for each background generation
    38|
    39|**Sizes:** `"1024x1024"` (square, default), `"1024x1536"` (portrait), `"1536x1024"` (landscape)
    40|
    41|**Background:** `"transparent"` for cutout images/mascots, `"opaque"` for scenes/photos, `"auto"` lets the model decide
    42|
    43|**Edit mode:** Pass `inputImages` array with URLs to edit/composite existing images
    44|
    45|**Tips for prompts:**
    46|- Be specific about style, colors, composition, and where it will be used
    47|- For app backgrounds: describe the mood, gradient, pattern
    48|- For illustrations: describe the subject, style (flat, 3D, watercolor), colors
    49|- Use `assetName` as a short snake_case identifier: `hero_banner`, `onboarding_bg`, `empty_state_illustration`
    50|
    51|**NEVER retry failed image generations.** If an image fails, the user sees the error on the card and can retry manually.
    52|
    53|NOTE: This is for "asset" type images only. For app icons, thumbnails, and App Store screenshots, use the existing `generateImage` tool with the appropriate `type` parameter.
    54|

(End of file - total 54 lines)

responseMessageContents.text:
Let me plan out this curb design company app. It needs:
- Home page with hero, services overview, CTA
- Book a service page
- Newsletter signup
- About us
- Contact us
- Service areas
- Types of designs
- Testimonials
- Reviews

Since this is a business website-style app, I'll use tabs for main navigation and stack screens for sub-pages. Let me think about the design:

- Inspiration: Premium contractor/design company feel. Think dark charcoal + warm gold/amber accents. Clean, modern, architectural feel.
- Tabs: Home, Services, Book, About, More (contact, reviews, etc.)


- I'll organize the structure with a tab navigator at the root level, then build out each section: Home with hero and featured work, Services showing design types, Book with a service form, About for company info, and a More tab for contact, service areas, testimonials, and reviews. I'm going with a sophisticated palette of dark slate and charcoal paired with warm amber and gold accents on cream backgrounds.

Setting up the file structure... Now I'm setting up the color palette with warm creams and forest greens, then starting to build out all the screens and mock data for the app.

responseMessageContents.text:
Let me build out this curb design company app. I'll create all the files needed. Let me plan the structure:

Color palette - sophisticated, premium feel:
- Background: #F7F3EE (warm cream)
- Card: #FFFFFF
- Primary: #2B3A33 (deep forest green)
- Accent: #C4955A (warm copper/gold)
- Text: #1C1C1E
- Subtle text: #8E8E93
- Border: #E8E2DA


- Dark sections: #1E2A24

I'm organizing the navigation with a home tab for the hero and featured work, services for design offerings, a booking form, about section, and a more menu that links to contact, service areas, testimonials, and reviews. I'll start by setting up the constants and mock data, then build out all the screens.

Now let me build out this elegant curb design company app. I'm envisioning a premium, architectural aesthetic — warm cream backgrounds with deep forest green and copper/gold accents, inspired by high-end home service apps like Houzz and architectural firm portfolios.

**Features for v1:**
- Home with hero banner, featured work, and newsletter CTA
- Services page showcasing curb design types
- Booking form for scheduling services
- About Us page with company story
- More menu linking to Contact, Service Areas, Testimonials, and Reviews

Let me start building:

responseMessageContents.text:
Your **CurbCraft Design** app is ready! Here's what's included:

**5 Tabs:**
- **Home** — Hero banner with animated entrance, stats bar (2,500+ projects, 15+ years, 4.9 rating), popular designs carousel, CTA banner, client testimonials preview, and newsletter CTA
- **Designs** — Full catalog of 6 curb design types (Classic Scroll, Modern Geometric, Natural Stone, Brick Pattern, Flagstone, Mower Edge) with features and "Get Quote" buttons
- **Book** — Complete booking form with personal info, design preference chips, scheduling (date + time slots), and notes — shows a success animation on submit
- **About** — Company story, core values grid, and team member cards
- **More** — Hub linking to all sub-pages

**Sub-pages (stack screens):**
- **Contact Us** — Phone/email/address cards + message form
- **Service Areas** — Expandable accordion for 5 states with city lists
- **Testimonials** — Full testimonial cards with quotes, ratings, and avatars
- **Reviews** — Rating breakdown chart + verified review cards
- **Newsletter** — Email subscription with benefits list and success state

**Design:** Warm cream backgrounds (#F7F3EE) with deep forest green (#2B3A33) and copper/gold (#C4955A) accents — premium, architectural feel with smooth animations and haptic feedback throughout.

responseMessageContents.title: Created a stylish website for a curb design and landscaping company.
responseMessageContents.description: Built a complete website for CurbCraft Design including sections for viewing design options, booking services, reading customer reviews, and finding contact information. It features an elegant design with easy navigation to help clients learn about the company's services and history.
